overrides.override_summary: Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act governs with respect to Illinois licensees. Provisions on governing law, jurisdiction, venue, and jury waiver do not waive Illinois FDD Act rights.
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchisees retain state law protections regardless of NY choice-of-law clause.

overrides.override_summary: Anti-waiver: any condition purporting to waive compliance with Illinois FDD Act is void.
overrides.why_it_matters: General release cannot waive Illinois franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law may supersede FA on termination and renewal.
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees may have additional termination/renewal protections beyond FA.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may sue in Maryland for claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law (3-year statute).
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia/NY venue clause does not prevent Maryland litigation for state law claims.

overrides.override_summary: General release does not release franchisor from Maryland FDD law liability. No statement waives claims under state franchise law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Release at transfer/re-licensing has limited effect in Maryland.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot terminate without good cause (30-day cure minimum).
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan law voids termination provisions that do not require good cause.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse to renew without fair compensation if term <5 years and franchisee prohibited from competing.
overrides.why_it_matters: May require compensation at non-renewal, though Motto's 22-year term likely exceeds the 5-year threshold.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require out-of-state arbitration or litigation.
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia/NY venue clause is void in Michigan.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require release waiving franchisee protections (except post-FA settlement).
overrides.why_it_matters: Release at transfer/renewal cannot deprive Michigan franchisees of statutory rights.

overrides.override_summary: 90-day termination notice with 60-day cure required. 180-day non-renewal notice required.
overrides.why_it_matters: Longer cure and notice periods than FA standard (10-30 days).

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages prohibited (Minn. Rule 2860.4400J).
overrides.why_it_matters: The extensive liquidated damages schedule in Item 6 may be unenforceable in Minnesota.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require out-of-state litigation, jury waiver, liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes.
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia venue, jury waiver, and LD provisions void in Minnesota.

overrides.override_summary: General release does not relieve franchisor from Minnesota Franchise Investment Law liability.
overrides.why_it_matters: Release cannot waive Minnesota franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law.
overrides.why_it_matters: NY law may provide termination rights not in FA.

overrides.override_summary: NY Franchises Law (GBL Art. 33, Secs. 680-695) may supersede inconsistent FA provisions. NY Franchises Law governs causes of action arising under it.
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law takes precedence over FA terms where inconsistent.

overrides.override_summary: General release required at renewal/transfer may not be enforceable under NY law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Release requirement potentially unenforceable in New York.

overrides.override_summary: ALL liquidated damages provisions deleted in their entirety.
overrides.why_it_matters: The entire LD schedule ($13,000/room, 60x royalty, etc.) is unenforceable in North Dakota.

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law supersedes and governs. NY choice-of-law overridden.
overrides.why_it_matters: ND law governs the franchise relationship, not NY law.

overrides.override_summary: Out-of-state venue provisions deleted. Must litigate in North Dakota.
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia/NY venue clauses void.

overrides.override_summary: Release does not relieve from ND Franchise Disclosure Act liability. Jury trial waiver deleted.
overrides.why_it_matters: Broad franchisee protections in North Dakota.

overrides.override_summary: Release does not apply to Rhode Island Franchise Investment Act claims.
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchise law claims survive release.

overrides.override_summary: Venue/governing law provisions restricting to out-of-state are void.
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot force RI franchisees to litigate in Virginia.

overrides.override_summary: Unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause (Section 13.1-564 Virginia Retail Franchising Act).
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional cause requirement for termination.

overrides.override_summary: RCW 19.100.180 may supersede FA on termination and renewal.
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington statute provides additional termination protections.

overrides.override_summary: Release excludes Washington FIP Act rights unless negotiated settlement with independent counsel.
overrides.why_it_matters: Boilerplate release at transfer/renewal cannot waive WA franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Washington FIP Act prevails in conflict of laws.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law takes precedence over NY choice-of-law.

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void against employees earning <$100K/year and independent contractors earning <$250K/year (RCW 49.62.020, 49.62.030). Cannot restrict franchisee from soliciting/hiring employees of same franchisor's other franchisees (RCW 49.62.060).
overrides.why_it_matters: During-term noncompete restrictions significantly limited in Washington. No-poach provisions void.

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation site must be in Washington or mutually agreed. Litigation may be brought in Washington.
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia venue does not apply to WA franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees limited to reasonable estimated or actual costs.
overrides.why_it_matters: $150,000 COO fee may be challenged if exceeds actual costs in WA.

overrides.override_summary: All disputes settled by arbitration (AAA/JAMS). Venue: Fairfax County VA. Seat: New York NY.
overrides.why_it_matters: Arbitration mandatory in Puerto Rico (not available in mainland US).
